_comment: Example AIP Grant Response — AIP spec §4.5.4. Constructed and signed by the Principal Wallet after Alice approves the grant ceremony. Delivered via POST to the callback_uri in the GrantRequest. The principal_token is a compact JWT whose payload conforms to principal-token.schema.json. Signature placeholder.

_principal_token_decoded._note: Decoded for reference only — not part of the actual GrantResponse object.

_notes.deployer_validation: On receipt the deployer MUST: (1) verify grant_request_id matches original, (2) verify nonce matches — mismatch = forgery, (3) status is 'approved' so proceed, (4) verify signed_at is plausible (not future, not >24h past), (5) decode and verify principal_token JWT signature against did:key:z6Mkha...do7 public key, (6) verify principal_token.sub = agent's AID, (7) use approved_capabilities to construct Capability Manifest.
_notes.full_approval: status='approved' means principal approved all requested capabilities. approved_capabilities matches requested_capabilities from the GrantRequest.
_notes.partial_example: If Alice had declined email.delete capability, status would be 'partial' and approved_capabilities would omit email.delete. The deployer MUST handle 'partial' without assuming full approval.
_notes.next_step: The deployer constructs the Registration Envelope using identity (pre-generated), capability_manifest (from approved_capabilities, signed by principal), principal_token (from this response), and grant_tier: 'G2', then submits to POST /v1/agents.
_notes.iss_field: principal_token.payload.iss = principal.id for root tokens (delegation_depth=0). The iss field was added in AIP spec v0.3.
